title: Nasal allergy follow-up
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

presenting_history.text: Lists sneezing, itching, rhinorrhoea, postnasal drip, congestion, smell change, eye symptoms, timing, and allergen or occupational triggers.
presenting_history.rationale: The exact DHA allergic-rhinitis history section supports interval change, nasal and eye symptoms, impact, associated features, relevant negatives, triggers, and concerns in UAE telehealth without assigning control or diagnosis.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Lists sneezing, itching, rhinorrhoea, postnasal drip, congestion, smell change, eye symptoms, timing, and allergen or occupational triggers.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Allergic Rhinitis

red_flags.text: Lists unilateral symptoms, visual change, epistaxis, fever, facial pain, purulent or bloody discharge, and systemic concerns.
red_flags.rationale: The exact DHA red-flag section was reviewed for workflow safety, but broad generic red-flag text was not treated as proof of any specific finding.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists unilateral symptoms, visual change, epistaxis, fever, facial pain, purulent or bloody discharge, and systemic concerns.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Allergic Rhinitis

examination.text: Supports clinician-performed general inspection, mouth-breathing observation, airflow assessment, anterior rhinoscopy, secretions, septal appearance, polyps, crusting, throat, and postnasal-drip documentation.
examination.rationale: The exact BSACI examination section supports clinician-performed general and ENT examination without auto-populating nasal findings.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-performed general inspection, mouth-breathing observation, airflow assessment, anterior rhinoscopy, secretions, septal appearance, polyps, crusting, throat, and postnasal-drip documentation.

source.title: BSACI guideline for the diagnosis and management of allergic and non-allergic rhinitis (Revised Edition 2017; First edition 2007)

exact_section.heading: Sections 8.1–8.3 — visual, nasal, oral, and upper-airway examination

investigations.text: Describes clinician-selected specific-IgE testing by skin-prick or serum when confirmation is relevant.
investigations.rationale: The exact DHA investigation section supports review of existing specialist or investigation information when available without ordering tests.
investigations.evidence_extract: Describes clinician-selected specific-IgE testing by skin-prick or serum when confirmation is relevant.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Allergic Rhinitis

investigations.text: Covers clinician-led allergen education, management options, structural obstruction, non-response, imaging decisions, and referral criteria.
investigations.rationale: The exact UAE management and referral section supports clinician-entered plan, safety net, follow-up, and questions only when discussed; no antihistamine, steroid, immunotherapy, or referral is generated.
investigations.evidence_extract: Covers clinician-led allergen education, management options, structural obstruction, non-response, imaging decisions, and referral criteria.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Allergic Rhinitis

sources_used.title: BSACI guideline for the diagnosis and management of allergic and non-allergic rhinitis (Revised Edition 2017; First edition 2007)
sources_used.jurisdiction: United Kingdom specialty guideline; international evidence requiring UAE allergy, ENT, prescribing, and referral adaptation

sources_used.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Allergic Rhinitis

source_limitations: Allergen, seasonality, symptom pattern, eye and asthma context, medicine use and response, examination, testing, diagnosis, control, treatment, referral, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
